title: Private API Caching, Representation ETags, Preconditions, and Invalidation · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A civic evidence team using keyboards, screen readers, narrow devices, and multiple signed-in clients. They need tenant-safe source and reader state, stable timelines, bounded explainable search, safe publisher content, runtime-documented APIs, privacy-safe caching, and conflict recovery. Planned artifact: Deliver Private Accessible Cross-Device Reader with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Require tenant scope in repository methods, SQL, keys, constraints, caches, tests, metrics, exports, support, and admin operations.
Implement forward and reverse keyset predicates aligned with sort, null policy, index, limit-plus-one, and output reversal.
Return bounded safe text snippets or sanitize controlled markers and validate every field before rendering.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Ts Tenant Repository Con…
steps.instruction:
project case — A project TypeScript team handles case 8865 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile private cache classes, representation validators, conditional reads, writes, and invalidation; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
